entries.title: Three Branches of Government
entries.content: Legislative (makes laws), Executive (enforces laws), Judicial (interprets laws). Separation of powers prevents tyranny.

entries.title: Constitution
entries.content: Supreme law defining government structure and rights. Many democracies have written constitutions amendable by defined process.

entries.title: Rule of Law
entries.content: Everyone, including leaders, is subject to law. Ensures fairness, predictability, and protection of rights.

entries.title: United Nations
entries.content: International organization (1945) promoting peace, security, and cooperation. HQ in New York; 193 member states.

entries.title: Voting and Democracy
entries.content: Citizens choose representatives or policies. Free elections require universal suffrage, secret ballot, and fair competition.
